text:
Why don't we talk about turtles?

There once was a wise old sea turtle who spent its days basking on lollipop-like sticks. Every morning, it stretched its whiskers and pondered the world. Once in a while, it would say something insightful like, "A well-spaced tree is a friend to all birds."

Our hero was known far and wide for its deep thoughts and beautiful shell ornamentation. One bright morning, the turtle decided that perhaps it didn't need to bask at all. On a whim, it hopped out onto the beach's edge and ventured into the lush forest.

Hours passed while the turtle explored. It discovered mushrooms, danced with squirrels, and even played hide-and-seek with an acrobatic squirrel. The thrill was unmatched, and it hadn't felt such joy since its last visit to a sunny patch.

Back on dry land, the turtle felt an unusual sensation. There was no warmth from its shell—it had fallen asleep amidst the leaf
